definition: means the Government of the State under whose authority the ship is operating.
means a coating, paint, surface treatment, surface, or device that is used on a ship to control or prevent attachment of unwanted organisms.
means the Marine Environment Protection Committee of the Organization.

means the gross tonnage calculated in accordance with the tonnage measurement regulations contained in Annex 1 to the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships, 19691, or any successor Convention.
a voyage by a ship entitled to fly the flag of one State to or from a port, shipyard, or offshore terminal under the jurisdiction of another State.
the length as defined in the International Convention on Load Lines, 19662, as modified by the Protocol of 19883 relating thereto, or any successor Convention.
the International Maritime Organization.
the Secretary-General of the Organization.

a vessel of any type whatsoever operating in the marine environment and includes hydrofoil boats, air-cushion vehicles, submersibles, floating craft, fixed or floating platforms, floating storage units (FSUs) and floating production storage and off-loading units (FPSOs).

is a body comprised of representatives of the Parties, Members of the Organization, the United Nations and its Specialized Agencies, intergovernmental organizations having agreements with the Organization, and non-governmental organizations in consultative status with the Organization, which should preferably include representatives of institutions and laboratories that engage in anti-fouling system analysis. These representatives shall have expertise in environmental fate and effects, toxicological effects, marine biology, human health, economic analysis, risk management, international shipping, anti-fouling systems coating technology, or other fields of expertise necessary to objectively review the technical merits of a comprehensive proposal.
A proposal that includes all the information required in Annex 3.
systems designed to prevent or reduce fouling on ships' hulls
in a manner that is excessive or unreasonable
held or kept in custody
an alteration to the terms of this Convention
a supplementary agreement attached to this Convention
systems that prevent or inhibit the growth of marine organisms on submerged surfaces
chemical substances that kill living organisms
document issued by an Administration or authorized person/organization certifying that a ship complies with the Convention regarding anti-fouling systems
The port where a vessel is registered.
The IMO number is a unique identification number assigned to a vessel by the International Maritime Organization.
An agreement between states that establishes rules and regulations governing a particular aspect of international relations.
Regulations aimed at preventing the use of harmful chemicals in anti-fouling systems on ships.
